vBRIEFInfo.description: Freeze-prep story P decomposed from GitHub issue #1912 (Tier-2 npm CLI legacy-layout refuse-preflight + signposts)

plan.title: feat(#1912 P): npm CLI legacy-layout refuse-preflight (two-step) + signpost-only stable-URL surfaces

narratives.Description: Freeze-prep story P of the #1912 capstone. Implements the Tier-2 runtime surface: the `npx @deftai/directive` CLI (init / update), fetched fresh at use-time, detects a LEGACY on-disk layout (git-clone / submodule / legacy `deft/`-prefixed / pre-v0.27 sentinel-only AGENTS.md / orphan `.deft/VERSION`) and REFUSES with the two-step recovery -- (1) run the frozen Go bridge installer, (2) re-run the npm path. The installed doctor / AGENTS / update-check surfaces are SIGNPOSTS ONLY: they carry a STABLE UPGRADING.md URL, never a baked command or version. Core principle (locked): never bake the upgrade command/version into the artifact being upgraded -- bake in a stable pointer resolved fresh. Today the npm deposit path hardcodes `legacy_layout: false` (init-deposit.ts / refresh.ts); this story makes that detection real and wires the refusal.
narratives.Origin: Decomposed from https://github.com/deftai/directive/issues/1912 (freeze-prep, agent-buildable subset). Independent of the Tier-0 SoT (story G) because the refusal points at a stable URL, not a baked version.
narratives.Overview:
## What to build

### Legacy-layout detection (npm CLI)
Replace the `legacy_layout: false` stub in `packages/core/src/init-deposit/init-deposit.ts` and `packages/core/src/init-deposit/refresh.ts` with a real detector that recognizes the legacy shapes: git-clone / submodule deposit, legacy `deft/`-prefixed paths, pre-v0.27 sentinel-only AGENTS.md (no managed-section), orphan `.deft/VERSION` (no `.deft/core/`). Reuse the manifest/AGENTS heuristics already in `packages/core/src/doctor/manifest.ts` (parseInstallRootFromAgentsMd, extractManagedSection, locateManifest) -- do NOT reinvent.

### Refuse-preflight (two-step)
When `directive init` / `directive update` detect a legacy layout, REFUSE (do not deposit/refresh) and print the two-step recovery:
  1. Download + run the frozen Go bridge installer (stage 1) -- point at the stable UPGRADING.md URL for the exact command (do NOT bake a version).
  2. Re-run `npx @deftai/directive init` (stage 2) once the layout is canonical-vendored.
Exit non-zero (needs-action). This is the active gate -- it runs fresh from npm at use-time.

### Signpost-only surfaces (stable URL)
- Installed `deft doctor`: when it detects a legacy layout, emit a signpost line carrying the STABLE UPGRADING.md URL (no baked command/version).
- UPGRADING.md (root + content/UPGRADING.md): document the two-step legacy->bridge->npm path, referencing the bridge generically (the actual pinned version is the Tier-0 SoT owned by story G; do NOT hardcode a number here -- describe it as 'the frozen final Go installer, see releases').
- AGENTS.md / update-check signpost text: stable URL only.

## Must NOT
- Bake a Go-installer version number or a literal upgrade command into any installed artifact (doctor / AGENTS / update-check) -- stable URL only.
- Touch the Tier-0 SoT module or the freeze/drift gates (story G owns those).
- Attempt to normalize the legacy layout from the npm side (that is stage 1's job -- npm REFUSES, it does not migrate).
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- packages/core/src/init-deposit/legacy-detect.ts (new): the legacy-layout classifier (reuses doctor/manifest.ts heuristics) + a typed result.
- packages/core/src/init-deposit/init-deposit.ts + refresh.ts: call the detector; on legacy, return a refuse result (drop the hardcoded `legacy_layout: false`).
- packages/cli/src/init-cli/init.ts + update.ts: on a refuse result, print the two-step + stable URL and exit non-zero.
- packages/core/src/doctor/checks.ts (+ constants.ts): legacy-layout signpost carrying the stable UPGRADING.md URL.
- UPGRADING.md + content/UPGRADING.md: two-step legacy->bridge->npm section (version-neutral).
- Tests: legacy-detect.test.ts (each legacy shape + the canonical-vendored negative), init/update refuse-path tests, doctor signpost test.
narratives.UserStory: As a consumer on a legacy (clone/submodule/pre-v0.27) Deft layout, when I run the npm CLI it should refuse cleanly with a two-step recovery pointing me at a stable URL, so I run the frozen Go bridge first and then the npm path -- without the npm CLI ever guessing at migration or baking a stale command.

items.title: real legacy-layout detection replaces the stub

narrative.Acceptance: When the on-disk layout is a legacy shape (clone/submodule/legacy-prefix/pre-v0.27/orphan VERSION), the detector reports legacy; canonical-vendored reports not-legacy.

items.title: npm CLI refuses legacy with the two-step

narrative.Acceptance: When directive init/update detect a legacy layout, they refuse (no deposit), print the two-step recovery, and exit non-zero.

items.title: signposts carry a stable URL, never a baked command/version

narrative.Acceptance: When doctor/AGENTS/update-check signpost the upgrade, they carry the stable UPGRADING.md URL with no hardcoded Go-installer version or command.

items.title: UPGRADING.md documents the version-neutral two-step path

narrative.Acceptance: When a user reads UPGRADING.md, the legacy->bridge->npm two-step is documented without a hardcoded bridge version number.

references.title: Issue #1912: build + freeze the last Go installer (parent capstone)

expected_outputs: npm CLI detects legacy layouts and refuses with a two-step stable-URL recovery; installed signposts carry stable URL only (no baked version); UPGRADING.md documents version-neutral two-step
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to #1912 Tier-2 corrected-design checklist + operator scaffold-now decision.
